exemplar.title:
To what extent have Netflixâs social media marketing strategies
impacted its sales and profits from 2020 to 2024?

To what extent have Netflixâs social media marketing strategies
impacted its sales and profits from 2020 to 2024?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear, repeated definition and linkage of âChangeâ to Netflixâs strategic evolution
Consistent connection of the key concept to every analytical model (7 Ps, Porter, Ansoff, financial ratios)
Strong contextual examples showing how Change drives specific organizational decisions
weaknesses: Occasional drift into descriptive narrative without analytical depth
Delayed explicit definition of Change early in the report
Minor lapses in explicitly tying each analytical section back to the concept
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a strong understanding of âChangeâ with clear analytical thinking and integrates the concept throughout most of the assessment. However, occasional descriptive passages and a later definition of the concept prevent award of the maximum band. A score of 4 appropriately reflects strong but not fully seamless integration.

strengths: Exactly five supporting documents provided, meeting the IB requirement
Documents are directly relevant (annual reports, industry articles, analytics) and properly cited
Sources supply surface data that supports most arguments
weaknesses: Limited depth and triangulation of sources
Lack of internal company materials or deeper qualitative evidence
No brief list of documents in methodology reduces transparency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The quantity and relevance of documents meet expectations for band 3, providing sufficient but not extensive depth or a broad range of perspectives. Hence, 3 marks out of 4 are justified.

strengths: Effective selection of varied tools and theories (7 Ps, Porter, Ansoff, ratios, regression)
Models are clearly chosen to address the research question on social-media impact
Overall toolkit demonstrates a sophisticated understanding of business analysis
weaknesses: Application is sometimes descriptive rather than evaluative
Generic overviews precede Netflix-specific application (e.g., Porter)
Regression assumptions and robustness metrics are not fully explained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While application occasionally lacks depth, the selection of tools is consistently effective and largely well-applied to Netflixâs strategy. To recognize the strong overall toolkit and its relevance, the criterion is awarded the top mark of 4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Broad use of quantitative and qualitative data (financial series, subscriber counts, follower growth)
Charts and tables are clear, correctly labelled, and support key arguments
Analysis links data trends to strategic outcomes and the concept of Change
weaknesses: Minor reliability issues (mixed units, estimated figures)
Analytical depth variesâsome sections (Porter, Ansoff) remain largely descriptive
Key metrics (e.g., regression RÂ², sentiment scores) are missing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis is mostly effective and sustained, with clear integration of data and strategy. Reliability and depth issues keep it from the highest band, so a score of 4 properly reflects the studentâs strong but imperfect evaluation.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with presented evidence
The research question is explicitly and directly answered
Findings are summarized coherently
weaknesses: Section restates earlier analysis rather than synthesizing new insights
Lacks precise quantification of the degree of impact
Could integrate reflections on tool limitations into the conclusions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusions align completely with the evidence and address the research question explicitly, warranting the maximum of 3 marks for this criterion.

strengths: Logical structure with numbered sections and a clear table of contents
Consistent flow from background through methodology to conclusions
Headings generally guide the reader effectively
weaknesses: Inconsistent heading hierarchy in places
Critical evaluation (tool limitations) appears after the conclusion rather than within
Research question could be more precisely specified in headings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report demonstrates an appropriate and coherent structure throughout, fulfilling all requirements for a full score of 2.

strengths: All required IB elements are present: word count, page numbers, references, figures and bibliography
Visuals and bibliography are formatted and included
Supporting document citations are provided
weaknesses: Citation formats vary and some entries are inconsistent
Several formatting errors in tables and footnotes
URLs and references occasionally break across lines or merge multiple links
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite numerous minor formatting and citation inconsistencies, the submission includes all IB presentation elements and thus merits the full 2 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure with numbered sections, sub-headings, TOC and coherent flow is evident, satisfying the âappropriate structure throughoutâ descriptor (band 2).

Documents are clearly relevant (annual reports for finance, industry articles for strategy, Keyhole social-media analytics) and are cited in footnotes.  Depth is moderate: most sources supply surface data rather than rich internal material and there is limited triangulation.  This aligns with ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the broader range/insight demanded for band 4.
Word count, references, page numbers, figures and bibliography are present, indicating that all required IB elements have been included; therefore band 2 is appropriate.

Exactly five supporting documents are identified and labelled, satisfying the 3-5 requirement.  Band 3 is chosen because the rubric allocates 3 marks (not 4) for the correct range when no additional distinction is provided under the ânumberâ strand.

Each tool is applied to Netflix and loosely connected to sales/profit outcomes, but analysis is sometimes superficial (e.g. the 7 Ps discussion remains descriptive, and regression assumptions are unexplained).  Application is therefore adequate and relevant, yet not consistently rigorous or fully effective, placing it in band 3.

Financial time-series, subscriber counts and follower data provide multiple quantitative data sets; visuals (tables, charts, regressions) are clear and correctly labelled.  The breadth and appropriateness meet the âsufficientâ descriptor.  Minor reliability issues (estimated 2024 figures, mixed units) prevent classification as âeffectiveâ (band 5).

The student links data trends to strategies and offers evaluative comments (e.g., cost leadership economies of scale linked to rising margins).  Analysis is mostly effective and usually refers back to Change, though integration lapses in places (e.g., Porter paragraph largely descriptive).  This matches band 4: âmostly effective analysis with some integration.â

The research question is explicitly answered (âpositively contributed â¦ to a great extentâ) with supporting rationale.  This meets the descriptor for band 3âthe maximum for the âanswerâ strand.

The toolkit is varied and appropriate for the RQ: 7 Ps focus on promotion, Porter for competitive context, Ansoff for growth, ratio analysis and regression for quantitative testing.  This constitutes an âeffective selectionâ as per band 4; no major models are missing for a study of marketing impact.
Conclusions repeat the main findings and align with presented evidence; no contradictory statements are observed, fulfilling full consistency.  Band 3 is the highest available for this strand.

Netflix is consistently at the centre of every argument: financial tables, subscriber charts, regression and qualitative discussion all relate directly to the organisation.  The analysis links evidence (e.g. profit-margin trend) to specific strategic moves (market penetration via SMM), demonstrating integrated connection.  Minor lapsesâsuch as generic definitions of Porter before applying itâmean that integration is not fully sustained, so band 4 is the best fit.

The student repeatedly defines and returns to the key concept of Change, linking it to Netflixâs evolution in social-media tactics, cost leadership and product innovation. References to âtransformativeâ or âadaptiveâ strategies appear in every analytical section (7 Ps, Porter, Ansoff, financial ratio commentary), showing a clear thread of conceptual thinking beyond a simple mention.  However, the discussion sometimes drifts into descriptive narrative (e.g. lists of platform tactics) without pushing the analytical implications of Change for each decision, preventing full seamless integration; therefore the work fits âstrong understanding with partially integrated analysisâ rather than the fully-integrated descriptor for band 5.

annotations.comment: The key concept âChangeâ is stated but not defined until later; introduce a concise definition here to strengthen conceptual clarity.
Consider refining the research question by specifying which profit metrics are included (e.g., operating income, net profit) to avoid ambiguity.
The table of contents is well-organized with clear page indicators, which enhances navigability of the assessment.
Footnotes in the background section vary in style; standardize citation format (e.g., authorâdate) throughout.
Link the background discussion of Change more explicitly to the research question here to reinforce conceptual integration.
The definition of Change in the conceptual lens is strong and well connected to transformation in Netflixâs strategies.
The methodology mentions using five supporting documents but does not list them here; include a brief list for transparency.
Ensure consistency in acronyms: replace âROâ with âRQâ to maintain clarity and avoid confusion.
The 7 Ps discussion introduces evidence but lacks interpretation of how increased followers translated into revenue changes.
Cross-reference Supporting Document numbering consistently; SD 5 here should match the methodology index.
Figure 1 presents follower growth clearly, but the caption could note the time frame and key percentage increase for immediacy.
Heading levels vary (h3 then h1 under Strategies); use consistent hierarchy to maintain clear structure.
The section on social listening describes the tool but gives no data; include actual metrics (e.g., sentiment scores) for depth.
Cite the source for the 44.2 % subscriber increase to support the claim rather than presenting it as an unreferenced statistic.
Introduce Porterâs model application directly with Netflix context rather than generic overview to improve relevance.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The Porter analysis is descriptive; deepen evaluation by linking specific generic strategies to quantitative outcomes.
Cross-reference Table 1 explicitly when discussing financial data here to guide the reader effectively.
The relationship between SMM and Porterâs strategies is asserted; consider adding an example or data to substantiate this link.
In the Ansoff section, the minor diversification strategy is described but its impact on Netflixâs growth is under-evaluated; expand discussion.
Footnote 10 merges two distinct URLs; separate and format each source clearly to improve reference accuracy.
Table 1 uses colspan="9" but the table has 6 columns; correct the colspan to match actual column count.
The chart Fig.4 does not highlight the recent market share decline; annotate or comment on that dip to strengthen analysis.
Data units mix thousands and millions without explicit clarification; standardize or clearly note units in headings.
Table 2 includes 2019 data although the RQ covers 2020â2024; justify including this earlier data or remove for consistency.
The regression graph lacks statistical measures such as RÂ² or p-values; include these to evaluate model robustness.
The regression table layout has repeated empty heading cells; adjust formatting so column headers align properly.
Include commentary on confidence intervals or error margins in regression interpretation to acknowledge data variability.
The interpretation here suggests causation from correlation; add a caveat to acknowledge that correlation does not imply causation.
The summary in conclusions section repeats earlier analysis; focus on synthesizing insights rather than restating findings.
The answer to the research question is explicit but could benefit from quantifying the extent of impact for precision.
The reflection on tool limitations appears after conclusions; integrate this critical evaluation into the main conclusion section.
Bibliography sections mix âSupporting Documentsâ and âOther Sourcesâ without consistent alphabetical order; reorder for readability.
Several URLs break across lines (e.g., page 18); ensure each reference entry is continuous for clarity.
Citation styles vary (italicization, use of quotation marks); standardize to one IB-approved referencing style.
The citation for the Business Management Syllabus Guide lacks an access date; add this to complete the reference entry.
Proofread for OCR artifacts (e.g., âÏfâ); ensure all titles and names are accurately transcribed.
